pmf_retention.note: 51-100 lifetime customers, ~$70-140 avg spend, one-and-done by construction (no accounts).

moat_defensibility.note: Prompt + PDF parser; free incumbents (Slidebean/SeedBlink/OpenVC); no switching cost or data asset.

growth_market.note: Real founder-fundraising demand, but niche economics accrue to free-funnel platforms.

tech_ip.note: Stack fits team perfectly; nothing proprietary; code unseen.

deal_terms.note: $17k = 17x last-month rev; verified ultra-micro AI comps clear at $50-500 or 1-5x annualized.

seller_adverse_selection.note: Selling at month 7, 'lack of time', soft validation claims, peak-month framing.

key_unknowns: Acquisition channel (decisive): where do the 51-100 customers actually come from? If founder network/FI mentors → value_transfers=false → MARGINAL cap and effectively dead for this team.
Monthly revenue curve for all 7 months — is $980 a peak (fundraising seasonality peaks Mar/Oct/Nov; a one-month spike would collapse the multiple).
Repeat-purchase rate — the difference between a product and a launch; credits with no accounts imply near-zero.
Revenue verification — P&L is seller-authored; needs Stripe read-only. 40%+ of small deals carry inflated figures.
The 'B2B subscription' claim vs pay-per-credit description — any actual recurring contracts (accelerators/universities)?
What 'validation from mentors associated with Stanford and Founder Institute' concretely is.
Entity/Stripe transfer logistics from Nepal; IP cleanliness (Lovable/Bolt-style boilerplate licensing).
OpenAI cost per report and margin durability at scale.
seller_questions: Where do your customers come from, channel by channel, with attribution for the last 3 months? (transfer_key_person / growth_lever_fits — deal-breaker: if it's your personal network or FI cohorts, the revenue doesn't transfer)
Will you grant read-only Stripe access to verify the $7k lifetime and $980 last-month figures? (revenue_evidence — required before any offer)
Month-by-month revenue since launch (all 7 months). (seasonality / is $980 a spike)
What % of customers have purchased credits more than once? (pmf_retention — the credit model's real retention)
The listing says B2B subscription but describes pay-per-credit — do any customers (accelerators, universities, consultants) pay recurring? Contracts? (unit_economics)
What exactly is the Stanford / Founder Institute mentor validation — names, program, written feedback? (seller_adverse_selection)
OpenAI API cost per review and monthly total — what's the true gross margin? (unit_economics)
What transfers: code, domain, brand, email list, Stripe account, any mobile app (React Native is listed)? From a Nepali entity or personal account? (ip_transferable / value_transfers)
Why sell a 45%-growth business at month 7 rather than run it — and would you accept an earnout tied to 6-month revenue retention? (seller_adverse_selection / deal structure)

scorecard_markdown:
## Acquisition Scorecard v2 — AI Pitch Deck Reviewer (Acquire 9mhboh63w4, unidentified)

**Verdict: PASS (provisional) — 43/100, completeness 52%. Asking $17,000 ≈ 6× the $3k ceiling (no financing), but the deal fails on merits before budget.**

A 7-month-old, solo-built (Nepal) AI pitch-deck-review credit service asking $17k on $7k lifetime revenue / $4k profit. Internally consistent numbers, but the category is structurally hostile: **the seller's own two named competitors (Slidebean, SeedBlink) give AI deck review away free as lead-gen**, OpenVC made it free for everyone, and the cheapest paid comp is $2.49/deck. The no-account credit flow is anti-retention by design — every month starts at zero.

### Dimensions (score/5 · confidence)
- Operability & product upside — 4 (med): MERN/Supabase/OpenAI wrapper, trivially operable for this team; upside capped by the category, not by engineering.
- PMF & retention — 2 (low): 51–100 lifetime customers ≈ $70–140 avg spend, one-and-done by construction (no accounts); "1–3% churn" is not meaningful for pay-per-credit.
- Unit economics — 2 (low): ~53% margin is typical thin-wrapper; WTP ceiling thin and falling vs free substitutes; CAC unknown because the channel is undisclosed.
- Moat & defensibility — 1 (high): prompt + PDF parser; zero switching costs, no data asset; free incumbents. Verified-bad, not unknown.
- Growth & market — 2 (med): founder-fundraising demand is real and structural, but the economics of THIS niche accrue to free-funnel platforms with attached monetization.
- Tech & IP — 3 (low): stack fits the team perfectly; nothing proprietary; code unseen.
- Transfer / key-person — 2 (unknown): acquisition channel undisclosed — if it's the founder's network/FI mentors, value doesn't transfer.
- Deal terms — 1 (high): $17k ≈ 17× last-month revenue; verified ultra-micro AI comps clear at $50–$500 absolute or 1–5× annualized; the ask prices a launch spike as durable.
- Seller & adverse-selection — 2 (med): selling at month 7 for "lack of time", soft "Stanford/Founder Institute mentors" validation, peak-month framing.

### Gates
- operable: TRUE · growth_lever_fits: UNKNOWN (channel undisclosed; seller's own growth plan = social/content/digital marketing — the team's declared weakness)
- ip_transferable / value_transfers / not_fragile: UNKNOWN · revenue_evidence: seller_claimed (P&L doc exists but unverified)
- Affordability: FALSE ($17,000 vs $3,000 ceiling, no financing)

### Figure reconciliation (Phase 2.5)
Numbers are internally consistent: 17/7 ≈ 2.4× "TTM", 17/4 ≈ 4.3× profit, 980×12 ≈ the claimed $12k "ARR". But: founded Dec 2025 → "TTM" = lifetime; "ARR" = run-rate annualization of one month; "45% annual growth" is unverifiable for a 7-month-old. The stated 2.4× uses the smaller lifetime base; against claimed run-rate it is 1.42×. The "B2B subscription" model claim contradicts the pay-per-credit description.

### Identification
Target NOT identified despite an adversarial 16-candidate sweep (product-space, Nepal-founder, cross-listing). Distinctive traits (Nepal, Stanford/FI phrasing, exact seven criteria) surfaced nowhere public; the listing is sitemap-visible as `9mhboh63w4` but noindex. Identity requires Acquire buyer access. Notable: Seedblink AND Slidebean both operate their own free deck-review tools — which is why the seller names them.
